title: centaurea nigra subsp. microptilon
intro: Centaurea nigra subsp. microptilon, also known as mountain knapweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. This perennial herb is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia, and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, such as wounds, skin infections, and digestive problems, and has also been used to reduce fever and alleviate pain. Centaurea nigra subsp. microptilon can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
